title: STEINS;GATE

notes: Ran perfectly out of the box, played to completion and acquired 100% of the steam achievements on a controller. Saves and quicksaves worked with no problem, remained stable after many launches and about 24 hours of play.
title: STEINS;GATE

notes: I have tried to get this game running over many weeks. Only now did I think to try it with my steam controller on and in use for the game. Without it the selection would wildly scroll threw the game ui, thus making it unplayable for me. after reading what others said I figured out it was due to the Dpad controls. If you turned them off before starting the game in settings it might work flawlessly. Just be aware.
title: STEINS;GATE

notes: I made it to one of the endings. Game ran perfectly, except one day where the game window didn't show up until I switched resolutions, but I don't know if this is Linux-related.
title: STEINS;GATE

title: STEINS;GATE

notes:
base game runs perfectly out of the box, but steam improvement patch (http://sonome.dareno.me/projects/sghd.html) didn't. Patch would not load until after navigating to "[Steam directory with game]/steamapps/compatdata/412830/drive_c/windows/system32" and running the following command with parts in brackets filled in: 


"STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=[Steam directory with game]/steamapps/compatdata/412830 [steam directory with proton]/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16/proton run winecfg"


and adding a new "native, then builtin" override for dinput8.
